Login Adventure Safety Guidelines Your safety is our priority. Please review the essential safety rules below for a secure and thrilling experience. Bungee Jumping Choose a certified and experienced operator Ensure double harness system (ankle + body backup) Check cord condition and proper weight calibration Avoid if you have heart issues, high blood pressure, or injuries Follow jump posture and instructor guidance carefully Do not jump under alcohol or medication influence Wear secure clothing and properly fitted shoes River Rafting Always wear a helmet and life jacket (PFD) Follow guide instructions at all times Learn basic commands (forward paddle, high side, get down) Never stand in fast-moving water (foot entrapment risk) Hold paddle correctly with T-grip Choose rafting grade based on experience level Avoid rafting in extreme weather or high water conditions Paragliding Fly only with licensed pilots and operators Check harness locks and canopy condition before takeoff Avoid flying in strong winds or bad weather Follow takeoff instructions (run properly, don’t sit early) Wear helmet and proper footwear Do not carry loose items during flight Avoid if prone to panic or severe motion sickness Zip Lining Ensure harness is properly secured and locked Double-check carabiners and pulley system Maintain correct body posture (lean back, legs slightly up) Do not touch the cable while moving Keep safe distance from other riders Follow landing instructions carefully Use gloves if provided Hot Air Balloon Choose licensed and experienced operators Fly only in safe weather conditions Attend and follow pre-flight safety briefing During landing, bend knees and hold safety ropes Stay inside the basket at all times Wear comfortable clothing and closed shoes Do not touch burner or flight equipment General Safety Guidelines Select verified operators with good reviews and certifications Read and understand all Created by: bucketlistt Date: 21 May 2026 If you've ever thought about river rafting in India, you've probably heard that Rishikesh is THE place to be. Surrounded by the beautiful mountains, this place goes way beyond just yoga or scenic views. The Ganga River flows through here, offering Rishikesh river rafting experiences ranging from a quick 9 km to a 100 km epic adventure. So, how do you pick the right distance for you? Read to know! Why River Rafting Distance Really Matters? Before jumping into the river rafting in Rishikesh, it's good to know why the distance of your rafting trip matters. So, here we've prepared a detailed guide for you. The 9 KM Adventure If you're a fresher to Rishikesh river rafting or just want a little feel of adventure, the 9 km stretch is perfect. This route usually takes about 1 to 2 hours, and the rapids are gentle. You get to chill while splashing some water around, feel the peace, and enjoy the scenery without getting too tired. It's perfect for a beginner-friendly river rafting experience. The 16 KM Adventure Next up is the 16 km stretch, a slightly longer river rafting in Rishikesh , taking 2–3 hours. The rapid here is moderate, giving a bit more adrenaline while still being safe. It's long enough to feel like a real adventure, but not so long that you're exhausted halfway through. The 25 KM Adventure For those who want adventure and nature vibes, the 25 km route usually lasts 4 to 5 hours. Rapids are more exciting here, and you often stop for a small snack or to take pictures of the stunning scenery. The 35 KM Adventure Feeling brave? The 35 km Rishikesh river rafting stretch is where the real fun begins. Lasting 6 to 7 hours, this ride has a mix of moderate to slightly challenging rapids. Along the way, you'll find calm stretches for floating and intense rapids that make your heart race. We'd recommend this one for people who've tried shorter distances before and want a full-day thrill. It's tiring but oh-so-rewarding. The 100 KM Adventure Now, this is not for the faint-hearted. The 100 km adventure is basically an epic. It takes 2 to 3 days to complete, with overnight camping along the riverbanks. You'll experience everything: calm stretches, challenging Grade IV rapids, and the most untouched, scenic parts of the Ganga. If you love nature, adventure, and a challenge, this route is something you don't want to miss out on! Tips To Follow Before You Go For River Rafting In Rishikesh Always choose the best rafting company . Check their licenses, experience, reviews or ask locals for recommendations. The long flights and beautiful views make it special. The place is also ideal for beginners as trained pilots guide the flights. Ladakh — High-Altitude Adventures Ladakh is a place that offers a special experience for lovers of adventure. The high mountains and vast roads are well suited to cycling and hiking.
